com.tonic.jaloc.impl.sets

Class PIntSet

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeCollection<PIntArray,PIntWriter>
      • com.tonic.jaloc.impl.sets.PIntSet
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class PIntSet
extends AbstractNativeCollection<PIntArray,PIntWriter>
    A growable native int hash set with open addressing and backward-shift deletion.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      PIntSet()
      Creates an empty set on the system allocator.
      PIntSet(long expectedElements)
      Creates an empty set presized for expectedElements on the system allocator.
      PIntSet(NativeAllocator allocator, long expectedElements)
      Creates an empty set presized for expectedElements on the given allocator.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean add(int value)
      Adds value.
      void clear()
      Empties the set.
      boolean contains(int value)
      Tests whether value is present.
      void forEach(java.util.function.IntConsumer consumer)
      Emits each present value in no particular order.
      boolean remove(int value)
      Removes value.

      • Methods inherited from class com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeCollection

        capacity, close, isEmpty, isOpen, size

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • PIntSet

        public PIntSet()
        Creates an empty set on the system allocator.

      • PIntSet

        public PIntSet(long expectedElements)
        Creates an empty set presized for expectedElements on the system allocator.
        Parameters:
        expectedElements - presizes the table
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if expectedElements is negative

      • PIntSet

        public PIntSet(NativeAllocator allocator,
               long expectedElements)
        Creates an empty set presized for expectedElements on the given allocator.
        Parameters:
        allocator - the allocator to source memory from
        expectedElements - presizes the table
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if expectedElements is negative

    • Method Detail

      • add

        public boolean add(int value)
        Adds value.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to add
        Returns:
        true if the set changed
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • remove

        public boolean remove(int value)
        Removes value.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to remove
        Returns:
        true if the set changed
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • contains

        public boolean contains(int value)
        Tests whether value is present.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to test
        Returns:
        true if present
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • forEach

        public void forEach(java.util.function.IntConsumer consumer)
        Emits each present value in no particular order.
        Parameters:
        consumer - the receiver
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if consumer is null
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • clear

        public void clear()
        Empties the set.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed
